by_eval.notes: NEW skill catches dependency direction violation explicitly (old skill framed as error handling). Both caught LoD method chains and circular dependency via refactoring. Neither flagged unnecessary exports this run (agent variance — iteration 1 new skill caught 2/3).
NEW skill continues to catch DI anti-pattern as architectural boundary violation. Perfect score on TS violations.
Zero false positives. Both versions correctly identified placeholder generateID() and nil-notifier risk.
Zero false positives on boundary checks. Missed flagging TODO comment in generateId as incomplete work signal — agent variance, not skill deficiency (skill text explicitly lists TODO/HACK/XXX).
